data.prompt_text: Two fire lookout towers are located at points B and C, separated by a distance of 42 miles. A fire is spotted at point A. The rangers measure the angles \angle B = 42.0^\circ and \angle C = 99.5^\circ. How far is the fire from tower B (distance AB)?
data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 42 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 42.0^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 99.5^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 42.0^\circ - 99.5^\circ = 38.5^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(99.5^\circ)} = \frac{42}{\sin(38.5^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{42\sin(99.5^\circ)}{\sin(38.5^\circ)}

c \approx 66.5

\text{We are given side } a = 580 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 101.9^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 34.6^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 101.9^\circ - 34.6^\circ = 43.5^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(34.6^\circ)} = \frac{580}{\sin(43.5^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{580\sin(34.6^\circ)}{\sin(43.5^\circ)}

c \approx 478.5

\text{We are given side } a = 410 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 104.9^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 24.2^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 104.9^\circ - 24.2^\circ = 50.9^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(24.2^\circ)} = \frac{410}{\sin(50.9^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{410\sin(24.2^\circ)}{\sin(50.9^\circ)}

c \approx 216.6

c \approx 168.3

\text{We are given side } a = 23 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 61.1^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 85.2^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 61.1^\circ - 85.2^\circ = 33.7^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(85.2^\circ)} = \frac{23}{\sin(33.7^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{23\sin(85.2^\circ)}{\sin(33.7^\circ)}

c \approx 41.3
